title: Harness Support
description: Compare you-agent-factory harness support across claude, codex, opencode, pi, cursor, and agy for Model Context Protocol (MCP), worktrees, loop, thinking controls, open source, and external models.
openingSummary: Harness Support is the you-agent-factory reference for which agent runtimes the factory drives and which features each harness covers.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: Harness Support compares factory-supported harnesses by feature coverage. Use it when you need a scannable answer to which agent runtimes you-agent-factory can drive and which capabilities — Model Context Protocol (MCP), worktrees, loop, thinking controls, open-source status, or external model support — each one covers.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: A harness is the agent runtime the factory drives, such as a coding agent under a shared factory process. Feature coverage means whether that runtime supports a capability the factory can use with it. This reference compares those harnesses by coverage; the full harness definition lives on the harness concept page.

supportMatrix.title: Support Matrix
supportMatrix.body: Compare factory-supported harnesses across MCP, worktrees, loop, thinking controls, open-source status, and external model support. Cell values are yes, no, or partial.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Start with the feature you need — for example MCP, worktrees, or external models — then scan that matrix row across harness columns. Prefer a harness marked yes for that feature; treat partial as incomplete or uncertain coverage, and skip no. Use the related links when you need the harness concept, worktree or loop ideas, thinking controls, CLI commands, or configuration details.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is a web harness-support matrix reference. It is not a full runner or provider field dump, not a sync of packaged CLI docs, not the harness concept glossary, and not an agent-factory comparison blog. Claims stay conservative when product evidence is incomplete: partial cells mark uncertain or incomplete coverage rather than inventing full support.
